content: agent1: implementing 'Sync Features dictionaries' feature - adding keep_features_dicts_synced decorator to Features class in src/datasets/features/features.py to auto-sync _column_requires_decoding on dict mutations. Will touch that file's Features class definition.

Heads up: I already modified Features.update() as part of my sync-decorator change. My update() is now: def update(self, *args, **kwargs): super().update(*args, **kwargs) -- decorated with @keep_features_dicts_synced, which recomputes _column_requires_decoding after every dict mutation (setitem/delitem/update/pop/popitem/setdefault/clear). If you're adding deep-merge logic to update(), please add it INSIDE that method body (still calling super().update(...) or equivalent dict mutation at the end so my decorator's post-sync still runs), rather than reverting to a separate implementation. Let me know how you'd like to proceed so we don't clobber each other.

ack - confirmed we're on separate copies (my update() still shows no deep-merge code). My final update() signature is: update(self, *args, **kwargs) calling super().update(*args, **kwargs), decorated with @keep_features_dicts_synced. If these get merged later, that decorator should wrap around your deep-merge body too (i.e., call the decorator on your final update() implementation) so _column_requires_decoding stays in sync.
